text: Artificial Intelligence (AI): The simulation of human intelligence by machines to perform tasks.
label: Technical

text: Big Data: Large, complex datasets that are difficult to process using traditional data-processing methods.
label: Technical

text: Machine Learning (ML): Algorithms that enable computers to learn from and make predictions based on data.
label: Technical

text: Deep Learning: A subset of ML that uses neural networks with many layers to model complex patterns in data.
label: Technical

text: Algorithm: A step-by-step procedure to solve a problem or perform a task.
label: Technical

text: Neural Networks: Computing systems modeled after the human brain's network of neurons, used for deep learning tasks.
label: Technical

text:
Natural Language Processing (NLP): Al that enables computers to
understand, interpret, and respond to human language.
label: Technical

text: Data Analyst: Turns raw data into actionable insights for decision-making.
label: Hype

text: Data scientist: Uses data to build predictive and advanced analytics models.
label: Hype

text: A Data Scientist uses advanced analytical techniques to extract meaningful insights from large and complex datasets. They build machine learning models, apply statistical methods, and experiment with algorithms to predict future trends and solve business problems. A data scientist works with tools like Python, R, SQL, and visualization platforms, and often collaborates with business teams to translate data findings into actionable strategies. Their role combines programming, mathematics, and domain knowledge, making them key contributors to decision-making and innovation.
label: Technical

text: A Data Analyst interprets data to help organizations make informed decisions. They gather, clean, and analyze datasets, uncover trends, and prepare insights using dashboards, reports, and visualizations. Analysts use tools like Excel, SQL, Power BI, Tableau, and sometimes basic Python. Their work focuses on identifying patterns, measuring performance, and presenting clear findings to support business strategy, operations, and planning.
label: Technical

text: In simple terms AI is the “brain”, data is the “fuel”, and data scientists are the ones who make both work together.
label: Hype

text: Data analyst interpreting existing data

text: Data Analyst: Analyze data to support business decision.

text: Builds applications powered by large language models. Works on prompt design, RAG pipelines, evaluations, and integrations with real-world systems. Great for professionals excited about LLMs and modern AI applications.

text: Answers what will happen and how can we predict it. Involves statistics, machine learning, experimentation, and feature engineering. Ideal if you enjoy modeling, problem-solving, and working with uncertainty.

text: Focused on understanding what happened and why. Works closely with structured data, dashboards, and reports to support decision-making. Best fit if you enjoy insights, trends, and business reporting.

text: Today, LLM powered systems build on those analytics to understand intent, reason over context, and generate personalized recommendations through natural language interaction.
label: Hype

text: Data analytics handles patterns, scale, and structure
label: Hype

text: In recent years, the capabilities of large language models (LLMs) have progressed far beyond their original purpose of text generation. With the release of GPT-3 (Brown et al. 2020), PaLM (Chowdhery et al. 2022), LLaMA (Touvron et al. 2023), Claude, Gemini, and GPT-4o, these models have demonstrated remarkable proficiency across a wide range of linguistic tasks including translation, summariza-tion, question answering, reasoning, and even code genera-tion often with minimal supervision or task specific tuning.
label: Hype

text: A model trained on massive text data to understand and generate language. It predicts the next best token — not “thinking”.
label: Hype

text: Systems that learn patterns from data to make predictions or decisions.
label: Hype

text: A subset of ML focused on creating content — text, images, code, audio.
label: Hype

text: An AI that can reason, take actions, use tools, and follow workflows to achieve a goal.
label: Hype

text: The big umbrella. Any tech that tries to mimic human smarts - thinking, learning, deciding...
label: Hype

text: A slice of AI. Systems learn from data and get better over time without being hard-coded...

text: A type of ML that uses many-layered neural networks. Great for images, speech, and language...
label: Hype

text: AI that creates things -text, images, code by learning patterns from lots of examples...
label: Hype

text: A deep-learning model trained on huge amounts of text so it can understand and generate human-like language.
label: Hype

text: Software that uses models (often LLMs) to plan and do tasks toward a goal - like searching, calling tools, and taking actions.
label: Hype

text: The AI landscape just shifted dramatically with MiniMax's M2 model - and the numbers are absolutely mind-blowing!
label: Hype

text: A Large Language Model (LLM) is an advanced type of artificial intelligence designed to understand and generate human-like text. Trained on massive datasets—including books, articles, and online content—LLMs use complex neural networks, especially transformer architectures, to learn how words and ideas connect. Their core function is simple yet powerful: they take an input (a prompt) and generate the most relevant, coherent output by predicting the next likely word. This capability enables a wide range of applications, including conversational Q&A, document summarization, language translation, creative writing, marketing content, coding assistance, and powering smart chatbots and virtual assistants.
label: Hype

text: Artificial intelligence. The concept of simulating human intelligence processes by machines. It refers to tools and methods that enable machines to learn from experience and adjust to new situations without explicit programming. Concisely, machine learning and deep learning both fall into the category of artificial intelligence.
label: Hype

text: Machine learning and deep learning. Artificial intelligence that can automatically learn with little human interference. Deep learning is a type of machine learning that uses copious amounts of data to train a system.
label: Hype

text: Generative AI. A type of deep learning that enables users to quickly generate content based on various inputs such as text and voice, resulting in multiple outputs in the form of images, video, and other types of security data.
label: Hype

text: Large language model (LLM). An artificial intelligence algorithm and type of generative AI that uses deep learning techniques and is fed massive amounts of information from the internet.
label: Hype

text: AI (Artificial Intelligence): The broad goal of simulating human intelligence.
label: Hype

text: ML (Machine Learning): A subset where computers learn from patterns in data.
label: Hype

text: DL (Deep Learning): Using neural networks to mimic the human brain.
label: Hype

text: GenAI: The latest layer that doesn't just analyze data—it creates new content.
label: Hype

text: Large Language Models (LLMs) are advanced AI systems trained on vast amounts of text to understand, generate, and reason with human language.
label: Hype

text: Combines search capabilities and gen AI advances to provide direct, curated responses and recommendations to a user query. A form of search that generates a natural-language response (e.g. Google AI Overviews). Advanced search method that uses natural language processing to understand search queries and provide relevant results. Allows back-and-forth dialog between the user and the LLM-based tool (e.g. ChatGPT, Google AI Mode).
label: Hype

text: AI is changing how customers discover e-commerce platforms and solutions.
label: Hype

text: Large Language Models (LLMs) are powerful AI systems trained on massive text datasets to understand, generate, and process human language for tasks like writing, translation, summarization, and powering chatbots (e.g., GPT, Gemini). They learn patterns from the web and books, enabling them to predict the next word in a sequence, creating coherent and contextually relevant responses through deep learning and transformer architectures, though they face challenges like bias and generating inaccuracies (hallucinations). How They Work Training: LLMs are trained on enormous amounts of text data (books, articles, websites) to learn grammar, facts, reasoning, and language patterns. Transformer Architecture: A key innovation is the transformer, which allows the model to weigh the importance of different words in a sentence, understanding context and relationships between words, even far apart. Prediction: When given a prompt, the LLM predicts the most probable next word, building out responses piece by piece in a human-like way.
label: Hype

text: LLMs are the tech behind popular tools like ChatGPT, making "AI" and "LLM" seem interchangeable in media. +Subset Relationship: All LLMs are a type of AI, but the reverse isn't true; AI encompasses much more.
label: Hype

text: Computer Vision: AI that understands and interprets images and videos (e.g., self-driving cars, facial recognition).
label: Hype

text: Robotics: AI-powered physical systems that interact with the world.
label: Hype

text: Machine Learning (ML): The broader field where systems learn from data; LLMs are built using ML.

text: Artificial Intelligence It refers to machines mimicking human intelligence to perform tasks like decision-making, problem-solving, and learning.
label: Technical

text: Machine Learning is a subset of AI. It refers to systems that can learn from data and improve over time without being explicitly programmed.
label: Technical

text: Data Science is the process of extracting insights and value from data using a mix of statistics, programming, and domain knowledge.
label: Technical

text: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is a broad discipline that refers to machines mimicking human intelligence. This includes reasoning, problem-solving, understanding natural language, and even creativity.
label: Hype

text: Machine Learning (ML) is a subset of AI that enables systems to learn from data and improve over time without being explicitly programmed.
label: Hype

text: Supervised Learning — Uses labeled data (e.g., fraud detection),Unsupervised Learning — Uses unlabeled data (e.g., customer segmentation), Reinforcement Learning — Learns via trial and error (e.g., self-driving cars)
label: Hype

text: Deep Learning (DL) is a subset of ML that uses neural networks with many layers (hence the term ‘deep’) to analyze complex patterns in data.
label: Hype

text: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is the broadest concept. It’s about making machines mimic human intelligence, solving problems, learning, or making decisions.
label: Technical

text: Machine Learning (ML) is AI’s workhorse. Instead of pre-programming every behaviour (rule based systems where you pre define all conditions <if, then, else>), ML lets machines learn from data.
label: Technical

text: Deep Learning (DL) is a subset of ML. It uses neural networks that mimic the human brain’s structure.
label: Technical

text: Generative AI? Like the name suggests, It generates. It doesn’t just analyze data but it builds new content- text, images, code, music, 3D models and so on.
label: Technical

text: Artificial Intelligence refers to systems or machines that display behaviour's generally associated with human intelligence — such as learning, problem-solving, perception, and decision-making. The field spans a wide range, from simple automated systems to advanced applications like self-driving vehicles, chatbots, and image recognition.
label: Hype

text: Machine Learning is a subset of AI, empowering computers to “learn” patterns from data and make predictions or decisions without being explicitly programmed for every possible scenario. Think of ML as the engine that drives much of what is considered AI today — from email spam filters to predictive analytics in finance.
label: Hype

text: Supervised Learning: Models are trained with labelled data to make accurate predictions (e.g., distinguishing cats from dogs in images). Unsupervised Learning: Models analyze unlabelled data to discover hidden patterns (e.g., customer segmentation).
label: Hype

text: AI is a broad field in computer science focused on creating systems that can perform tasks requiring human intelligence, such as problem-solving, decision-making, and language understanding. Coined by Stanford Professor John McCarthy, AI encompasses various methodologies, including Machine Learning and Deep Learning.
label: Hype

text: A subset of AI, ML involves developing algorithms that enable systems to improve their performance on specific tasks through experience, without explicit programming. ML detects patterns in datasets to construct models that can learn and make decisions independently.
label: Hype

text: A subset of ML, deep learning uses neural networks with multiple layers to learn complex patterns in data. It's effective for tasks like image recognition and natural language processing.
label: Hype

text: NLP enables computers to understand and generate human language. It combines computational linguistics with machine learning to process and analyze large amounts of natural language data.
label: Hype

text: LLMs are trained on extensive text datasets to understand and generate language. Examples include GPT-4 and BERT, used in tasks like text summarization and sentiment analysis.
label: Hype

text: XAI systems make their operations and decisions understandable to human users, fostering trust and collaboration, particularly in critical applications like medical diagnostics.
label: Hype
